RIGOURATE: Quantify degree of overstatement in scientific claims using a continuous score [0,1], with evidence retrieval from full paper body
CESE_OCN: Calibrate evidence sufficiency for claim strength using a four-class relation taxonomy (supported, mild_scope_overclaim, strong_action_overclaim, contradiction_candidate)
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN does relation typing; RIGOURATE does scalar severity. Scalar may not directly separate mild from strong action overclaim.

RIGOURATE: Author-written claims from abstract and introduction of ICLR/NeurIPS papers
CESE_OCN: Generated claim variants instantiating specific evidence-sufficiency relations, paired with real evidence spans
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN uses controlled counterfactual claims; RIGOURATE uses natural claims. Realism risk differs.

RIGOURATE: Full paper body: text paragraphs, figures, tables (multimodal)
CESE_OCN: Real evidence spans from scientific papers (text only in current version)
implication_for_cese_ocn: Both use paper-internal evidence. RIGOURATE is multimodal; CESE_OCN is text-only.

RIGOURATE: Fine-tuned multimodal reranker (Stage 1)
CESE_OCN: Not present; evidence is provided
implication_for_cese_ocn: RIGOURATE has retrieval component; CESE_OCN assumes evidence is given.

RIGOURATE: Continuous scalar score [0,1]
CESE_OCN: Four-class relation label
implication_for_cese_ocn: Core representational difference. Scalar compresses severity; taxonomy separates relation types.

RIGOURATE: Not isolated as a separate class
CESE_OCN: Isolated as strong_action_overclaim (the high-risk boundary class)
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN targets high-risk action overclaim specifically; RIGOURATE treats all overstatement uniformly.

RIGOURATE: Treated as a rare edge case; claims rarely contradicted by internal evidence
CESE_OCN: Explicitly modelled as contradiction_candidate class with a separate detector
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN has a dedicated contradiction route; RIGOURATE does not isolate contradiction.

RIGOURATE: 8 LLM annotators + peer-review calibration + human evaluation
CESE_OCN: LLM pre-annotation + author screening (silver); pre-registered gold protocol pending
implication_for_cese_ocn: Both use LLM annotators. RIGOURATE uses review comments for calibration; CESE_OCN does not.

RIGOURATE: 10K+ claim-evidence sets, 872 papers (659 ICLR + 213 NeurIPS)
CESE_OCN: 444 pairs, 111 evidence groups, 6 domains
implication_for_cese_ocn: RIGOURATE is larger scale; CESE_OCN is smaller but controlled.

RIGOURATE: Peer-review comments used as calibration signal
CESE_OCN: Not used
implication_for_cese_ocn: RIGOURATE leverages review text; CESE_OCN does not.

RIGOURATE: Fine-tuned multimodal overstatement scorer with justification
CESE_OCN: Mixed relation-routing framework R4: contradiction detector + scope-calibration residual classifier + strong-action escalation route
implication_for_cese_ocn: RIGOURATE is a single regressor; R4 is a structured router with three components.

RIGOURATE: Brief natural-language justification alongside score
CESE_OCN: Class label only (no natural-language justification)
implication_for_cese_ocn: RIGOURATE produces reasoning text; CESE_OCN does not.

RIGOURATE: Retrieval metrics + regression-style scoring metrics (specific names not extractable from partial paper text)
CESE_OCN: Macro-F1, per-class positive-F1, precision, recall, FP/TP ratio, ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, threshold sensitivity
implication_for_cese_ocn: Both report retrieval/scoring quality. CESE_OCN has more screening-oriented metrics.

RIGOURATE: Zero-shot base reranker / multimodal models; strong baselines from prior scientific claim verification work
CESE_OCN: LLM judges (GPT-5.5, DeepSeek-V3), NLI/HCM features, flat4 baseline, R4 frozen
implication_for_cese_ocn: Different baseline families. RIGOURATE compares to retrieval/scorer baselines; CESE_OCN compares to LLM judges.

RIGOURATE: Targeted human evaluation of annotation quality
CESE_OCN: Pre-registered gold adjudication protocol (§VII) with frozen success/downgrade criteria
implication_for_cese_ocn: Both reference human validation. CESE_OCN has a pre-registered protocol; RIGOURATE has targeted evaluation.

RIGOURATE: Not pre-registered; human evaluation as validation
CESE_OCN: Pre-registered with frozen success/downgrade criteria (§VII)
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN has stronger pre-registration; RIGOURATE does not.

RIGOURATE: Not present (natural claims from real papers)
CESE_OCN: Proposed gold-realism audit (because SimClaim claims are generated counterfactuals)
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN has realism risk; RIGOURATE does not.

RIGOURATE: General overstatement (any rhetorical exaggeration)
CESE_OCN: High-risk action/deployment/safety/policy overclaims specifically
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN is screening-oriented for high-risk overclaims; RIGOURATE is broad-coverage.

RIGOURATE: Not publicly released (placeholder [Github/HF Link] in arXiv v2)
CESE_OCN: SimClaim silver data available in repo
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN data is reproducible; RIGOURATE data is not.

RIGOURATE: Not publicly released
CESE_OCN: CESE_OCN/R4 scripts available in repo
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN is reproducible; RIGOURATE is not.

RIGOURATE: Not publicly released
CESE_OCN: R4 frozen thresholds + model artifacts in repo
implication_for_cese_ocn: CESE_OCN is reproducible; RIGOURATE is not.

RIGOURATE: Operationalises evidential proportionality for transparent scientific communication
CESE_OCN: Fine-grained relation-type decomposition and high-risk action-overclaim screening on the existing scientific overstatement / evidence-force calibration track
implication_for_cese_ocn: RIGOURATE is broad-coverage scalar; CESE_OCN is fine-grained relation typing. Complementary, not competing.
